issue_title: Invalid client data in /login-actions/authenticate causes an uncaught server error and a HTTP 500 response code
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

authentication

### Describe the bug

When a client requests `/login-actions/authenticate` with invalid length `client_data` parameter, it causes an uncaught server error and results in an HTTP 500 response code.

### Version

25.0.2

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

When such request is handled, the server should respond with HTTP 400 (invalid request). This could be achieved by e.g.:


Catching the `Illegal base64url string` exception thrown from [encodeBase64UrlToBase64](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/ecbd856176fa81203be0fe07464299d39883e9f8/common/src/main/java/org/keycloak/common/util/Base64Url.java#L57) in [decodeClientDataFromParameter](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/ecbd856176fa81203be0fe07464299d39883e9f8/server-spi-private/src/main/java/org/keycloak/protocol/ClientData.java#L99) and then gracefully responding with HTTP 400.


Or first check the parameter for validity (length and characters in allowed range) and only do the conversion if the validation is OK.




### Actual behavior

Server respoinds with HTTP 500 error and an error is logged.

Here's the stack trace
```
org.keycloak.services.error.KeycloakErrorHandler Unexpected error when authenticating client: java.lang.RuntimeException: Illegal base64url string!
	at org.keycloak.common.util.Base64Url.encodeBase64UrlToBase64(Base64Url.java:71)
	at org.keycloak.common.util.Base64Url.decode(Base64Url.java:32)
	at org.keycloak.protocol.ClientData.decodeClientDataFromParameter(ClientData.java:104)
	at org.keycloak.services.resources.SessionCodeChecks.<init>(SessionCodeChecks.java:99)
	at org.keycloak.services.resources.LoginActionsService.checksForCode(LoginActionsService.java:207)
	at org.keycloak.services.resources.LoginActionsService.authenticate(LoginActionsService.java:329)
	at org.keycloak.services.resources.LoginActionsService$quarkusrestinvoker$authenticate_d833d327ad1940df8a3a3360cd1b0c741f4e7726.invoke(LoginActionsService.java:Unknown Source)
	at org.jboss.resteasy.reactive.server.handlers.InvocationHandler.handle(InvocationHandler.java:29)
	at io.quarkus.resteasy.reactive.server.runtime.QuarkusResteasyReactiveRequestContext.invokeHandler(QuarkusResteasyReactiveRequestContext.java:141)
	at org.jboss.resteasy.reactive.common.core.AbstractResteasyReactiveContext.run(AbstractResteasyReactiveContext.java:147)
	at java.util.concurrent.Executors$RunnableAdapter.call(Executors.java:572)
	at java.util.concurrent.FutureTask.run(FutureTask.java:317)
	at io.quarkus.vertx.core.runtime.VertxCoreRecorder$14.run(VertxCoreRecorder.java:582)
	at org.jboss.threads.EnhancedQueueExecutor$Task.run(EnhancedQueueExecutor.java:2513)
	at org.jboss.threads.EnhancedQueueExecutor$ThreadBody.run(EnhancedQueueExecutor.java:1538)
	at org.jboss.threads.DelegatingRunnable.run(DelegatingRunnable.java:29)
	at org.jboss.threads.ThreadLocalResettingRunnable.run(ThreadLocalResettingRunnable.java:29)
	at io.netty.util.concurrent.FastThreadLocalRunnable.run(FastThreadLocalRunnable.java:30)
	at java.lang.Thread.run(Thread.java:1583)
```

### How to Reproduce?

Example request:
`GET /login-actions/authenticate?client_id=my-users&tab_id=3N9L4_Ydops&client_data=eyJydSI6Imh0dHBzOi8vZXhhb`
Note that the length of client data is 25 characters, and 25 % 4 = 1. This causes an error being thrown from `encodeBase64UrlToBase64`:
```java
public static String encodeBase64UrlToBase64(String base64Url) {
    String s = base64Url.replace('-', '+'); // 62nd char of encoding
    s = s.replace('_', '/'); // 63rd char of encoding
    switch (s.length() % 4) // Pad with trailing '='s
    {
        case 0:
            break; // No pad chars in this case
        case 2:
            s += "==";
            break; // Two pad chars
        case 3:
            s += "=";
            break; // One pad char
        default:
            throw new RuntimeException(
                    "Illegal base64url string!");
    }

    return s;
}
```

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Handle ClientData parsing errors in SessionCodeChecks gracefully

- Move ClientData parsing out of SessionCodeChecks ctor
- Respond with a bad request if invalid client data is presented

Closes #32515

Signed-off-by: Thomas Darimont <thomas.darimont@googlemail.com>
Signed-off-by: Alexander Schwartz <aschwart@redhat.com>
Co-authored-by: Alexander Schwartz <aschwart@redhat.com>

commit_message:
Avoid NPE if realm configuration contains invalid required action configuration (#32649)

* Avoid NPE if realm configuration contains invalid required action configuration

If users removed implementations or renamed the provider id of a required action, then the realm configuration might contain dangling references to required actions.
If we then try to find the RequiredActionFactory to determine the if the required action is configurable then NPE is thrown. This PR prevents the NPE with a guard clause.

Fixes #32624

Signed-off-by: Thomas Darimont <thomas.darimont@googlemail.com>

* Log a warning if required action with missing provider is detected.

Signed-off-by: Thomas Darimont <thomas.darimont@googlemail.com>

---------

Signed-off-by: Thomas Darimont <thomas.darimont@googlemail.com>

issue_title: Invalid migration export for empty database
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

Using the [Setting JPA provider configuration option for migrationStrategy](https://www.keycloak.org/server/db) docs, generated a migration export with an empty database. The resulting SQL script contained duplicate CREATE TABLE statements for databasechangelog tables.

I removed the duplicate statement and used it to create a schema. Afterwards, started up keycloak with migrationStrategy set to validate. The database user, for keycloak, didn't have any DDL privileges. At startup, it tried to create databasechangeloglock tables, for which there wasn't a CREATE TABLE statement in the migration export. Keycloak failed to started due to the lack of DDL privileges.

Database: PostgreSQL 14
KC_DB_SCHEMA set to a non-public schema.

Looking at this part of migration export, https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/8ce8a4ba089eef25a0e01f58e09890399477b9ef/model/jpa/src/main/java/org/keycloak/connections/jpa/updater/liquibase/LiquibaseJpaUpdaterProvider.java#L214 , shouldn't it create loglock table instead of log table? 

### Version

25.0.4

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

The migration export should have CREATE TABLE statement for databasechangeloglock table. 

### Actual behavior

The migration export contains duplicate CREATE TABLE statement for databasechangelog table. 

### How to Reproduce?

Follow the steps mentioned under description. 

### Anything else?

No

issue_title: Multiple bugs in the experimental UiTabProvider / UiTabProviderFactory
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

There are 4 bugs that are related to the form fields within UiTabProvider / UiTabProviderFactory, with the steps to reproduce them are included in `How to Reproduce` info below:

BUG 1: The value of the form field of type `script` is not captured.
BUG 2: Clicking the `Save` button multipe times inside the custom tab page registers as `onCreate` events until the page is reloaded.
BUG 3: The custom tab page displays error upon reload if there is saved a script field.
BUG 4: Modifying the form fields will not capture the new values of all fields except for string type. 

### Version

25.0.4 (Docker image)

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

BUG 1: The value of the form field of type `script` should be captured.
BUG 2: Clicking the `Save` button multipe times inside the custom tab page should register as `onCreate` event for the first click only, while the subsequent clicks should register as update events.
BUG 3: The custom tab page should not display error upon reload even if there is saved a script field.
BUG 4: Modifying the form fields should capture the new values of all field types. 

### Actual behavior

BUG 1: The value of the form field of type `script` is not captured.
BUG 2: Clicking the `Save` button multipe times inside the custom tab page registers as `onCreate` events until the page is reloaded.
BUG 3: The custom tab page displays error upon reload if there is saved a script field.
BUG 4: Modifying the form fields will not capture the new values of all fields except for string type. 

### How to Reproduce?

1. Download [this TestUiTabProvider.java](https://gist.github.com/MLukman/037490697718faf0c1356d3772e92f3d) and embed it in an empty/existing Keycloak provider codebase with the correct namespace folder path. 
2. Register the full classpath inside `META-INF/services/org.keycloak.services.ui.extend.UiTabProviderFactory`. 
3. Compile the provider as a JAR and put it into Keycloak installation's providers folder and run Keycloak build command with `--features="declarative-ui"` flag.
4. Restart/Start Keycloak with the logging streamed to stdout.
5. Go to the Admin console, navigate to the `Realm settings` page (any realm) and find the custom tab `Test UI Tab`.
6. Enter any values in the form fields and click `Save`.
7. Go to the logging and notice that the "ONCREATE" is displayed along with the field values except for script field is null -- **BUG 1**
8. Return to the admin console, modify the fields and click `Save`.
9. Go to the logging and notice that event is still `ONCREATE` while it should be an update -- **BUG 2**
10. Return to the admin console and refresh the browser (F5).
11.  Notice that the `Test UI Tab` cannot load anymore with errors either `ee.create is not a function` or `k.charCodeAt is not a function` (with more probability to get the former than the latter) -- **BUG 3**
12. Now, go back to the source code for `TestUiTabProvider.java` and remove the lines that define the script field within `getConfigProperties()`. Recompile, re-add and rebuild the provider before restarting the Keycloak installation like steps 3-4 above.
13. Navigate back to the `Test UI Tab` (it should load fine now)
14. Modify the fields and click `Save`.
15. Go to the logging and notice that the event `ONUPDATE` is now correctly detected but only the string field new value is displayed while the list and text fields do not display the new value -- **BUG 4**

### Anything else?

To repeat the reproduction of BUG 1 & BUG 2, just create new realms and repeat the steps in the custom tab there.

issue_title: The set value ‘Default Admin-Initiated Action Lifespan’ has no effect on the ‘Credential Reset’ form
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

Even after override the value 'Default Admin-Initiated Action Lifespan' under 'Realm settings/Tokens', '12 Hours' is always preset in the form for the manual sending of a 'Credential Reset' mail.

This is probably caused by function **getActionTokenGeneratedByAdminLifespan()**, as a fixed value is always returned here:
https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/f2ff485b7fb96b6ea3ed598a6a3abff0f4b7bdda/model/jpa/src/main/java/org/keycloak/models/jpa/RealmAdapter.java#L611

However, the new lifespan value is used correctly when an email is initiated via the API.

### Version

25.0.4

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Admin initiated actions form should use the override lifespan value.

### Actual behavior

Admin initiated actions form always set lifespan to 12 hours as default.

### How to Reproduce?

- Set 'Default Admin-Initiated Action Lifespan' at 'Realm settings / Tokens' to any value other than 12 hours
- Go to 'Users details / Credentials' for any user
- Click on 'Credentials Reset'

### Anything else?

Override value is correctly used by initiate an email via the API

PUT /admin/realms/{realm}/users/{id}/execute-actions-email

issue_title: OpenAPI spec: Missing attributes in ClientPolicyConditionRepresentation and ClientPolicyExecutorRepresentation schemas
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/api

### Describe the bug

[RapiDAST (Rapid DAST)](https://github.com/RedHatProductSecurity/rapidast) is an open-source security testing tool that automates Dynamic Application Security Testing (DAST) and seamlessly integrates security into your development workflow.

It supports OpenAPI v2 (formerly Swagger) and v3 schemas, using them to define endpoints and their corresponding parameters.

However, when importing the OpenAPI descriptor from the Keycloak documentation, some errors were identified, which are also visible when running the [Swagger Validator tool](https://validator.swagger.io/). These errors prevent the security team from integrating the descriptor with the tool.



### Version

<= 25.0.4

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Import the OpenAPI descriptor in the Swagger Validator tool and see no errors.

### Actual behavior

When the OpenAPI descriptor in the Swagger Validator tool is imported, we see the [following errors](https://validator.swagger.io/validator/debug?url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.keycloak.org%2Fdocs-api%2Flatest%2Frest-api%2Fopenapi.json):

```
{
  "messages": [
    "attribute components.schemas.ClientPolicyConditionRepresentation.items is missing",
    "attribute components.schemas.ClientPolicyExecutorRepresentation.items is missing"
  ],
  "schemaValidationMessages": [ ]
}
```

### How to Reproduce?

1. Copy the link to the OpenAPI definition from https://www.keycloak.org/docs-api/latest/rest-api/openapi.json.
2. Paste it into https://validator.swagger.io/.
3. Scroll to the bottom of the page, and you should see a button labeled "Invalid."
![Screenshot from 2024-09-02 15-44-08](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/17f71c04-083e-40fe-96a9-66c8bb76d008)
4. Click on the button to see the error messages.

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: InvalidDestination Error for IDP-initiated SSO with Keycloak behind a Reverse Proxy
issue_body:
👍
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

saml

### Describe the bug

I have been playing with Keycloak behind an Nginx proxy which terminates TLS. I use Okta as my IDP and have tried both SP-initiated and IDP-initiated modes. I have read some blogs around this, but none talk about this specific setup and error - except for one person who did not provide any solution.

I see that the SP-initiated flow works fine with my setup and the [getExpectedDestination](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/d63c0fbd138ab0a27f3bb0833ae92e3211146c5c/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/broker/saml/SAMLEndpoint.java#L815) function returns the expected Destination properly with Scheme, host, and port similar to the original URL (before TLS termination - what Okta reaches out to).

However, the for IDP-initiated flow, the same function [getExpectedDestination](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/d63c0fbd138ab0a27f3bb0833ae92e3211146c5c/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/broker/saml/SAMLEndpoint.java#L815) returns the Destination that is "After" the termination (http - not https).

After some tracing of code, I see that the difference between SP and IDP-initiated flows is that the clientId is `null` for SP-initiated flow and therefore the destination is computed with this line of code
```Urls.identityProviderAuthnResponse(session.getContext().getUri().getBaseUri(), providerAlias, realm.getName()).toString();```
However, for IDP-initiated flow, I have a client that I go thru and therefore the clientId is NOT `null` and the destination is computed with the following line of code
```session.getContext().getUri().getAbsolutePath().toString();```

I see that SP-initiated flow works because it is relying on the BaseUri part of the context which is correctly set to the external https URI However, getAbsolutePath() part of the evaluation for IDP-initiated flow returns the 'http' URI.

I think it is a bug in the following line
```session.getContext().getUri().getAbsolutePath().toString();```

The destination is supposed to be built similar to how it is done when `clientId == null`

### Version

23.0.7

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

The `getExpectedDestination` function should return the URI that is before the proxy (just like it does when clientID is null).
In this case, the URI with HTTPS and port number used before the reverse proxy. 

### Actual behavior

The returned URI uses the scheme and port AFTER the reverse proxy. In my case, it uses HTTP and 80 as the port.

### How to Reproduce?

Put a TLS-terminating reverse proxy (like NGINX) in front of Keycloak and then set up an IDP-initiated SAML flow with a client defined. Then used an external IDP like Okta to connect directly. The destination URI will not match as the external IDP will put the public-facing address (example - https://auth.example.com:8443/realms/myrealm/protocol/saml/clients/myapp-saml) in the SAML assertion as the destination.
During the handling of the SAML response, the code under function `getExpectedDestination` will compute the expected destination URI as `http://auth.example.com/realms/myrealm/protocol/saml/clients/myapp-saml` if you have forwarded the host in NGINX using 
`proxy_set_header HOST            $host;`

You can see some setup steps for IDP-initiated flow in this blog: https://www.lisenet.com/2020/keycloak-with-okta-idp-initiated-sso-login/

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Fix for Issue# 32622 (https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/32622)
The expected Destination Path needs to properly point to the client that is created for IDP-initiated SSO flow. This is especially an issue when Keycloak is behind a reverse proxy that terminates TLS.

Signed-off-by: Manish Mehta <ManishMehta@users.noreply.github.com>

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

**Summary:** 
After performing a stepwise migration of a Keycloak server from version 21 to the latest version (25), the "Authentication" link in the admin portal causes a 400 Bad Request error. 

**Reproduction Rate:**

100% on upgraded instances (from version 21 to 25) with existing database data.
0% on fresh Keycloak instances that follow the same upgrade path without existing data.

commit_message:
Avoid NPE if realm configuration contains invalid required action configuration (#32649)

* Avoid NPE if realm configuration contains invalid required action configuration

If users removed implementations or renamed the provider id of a required action, then the realm configuration might contain dangling references to required actions.
If we then try to find the RequiredActionFactory to determine the if the required action is configurable then NPE is thrown. This PR prevents the NPE with a guard clause.

Fixes #32624

Signed-off-by: Thomas Darimont <thomas.darimont@googlemail.com>

* Log a warning if required action with missing provider is detected.

Signed-off-by: Thomas Darimont <thomas.darimont@googlemail.com>

issue_title: RP-Initiated logout using `POST` method fails in cross-origin setup
issue_body:
As part of [the specification](https://openid.net/specs/openid-connect-rpinitiated-1_0.html) we have implemented the `POST` method of RP-Initiated Logout under #12228, and support for Keycloak JS was added under #25348. However, when the RP (client) is on a different origin than the OP (OpenID Provider) this method fails. The reason for this is that the `POST` endpoint does not set the [`Access-Control-Allow-Origin` CORS header ](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/CORS/Errors/CORSMissingAllowOrigin)required for allowing these sorts of requests.

**Steps to reproduce**

I've [set up a little project](https://github.com/jonkoops/kjs-playground) that I use for testing Keycloak and Keycloak JS in cross-origin scenarios which I will use for convenience of reproducing this issue. Follow the steps outlined in the README and then add `logoutMethod: 'POST'` to the options provided to the `init()` call to Keycloak JS in `src/main.ts`.

**Proposed solution**

When calling the `POST` endpoint the `Access-Control-Allow-Origin` header should be added to the response to allow cross-origin requests. Only valid web origins configured for the client should be allowed at any time. There is already [existing code](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/557d7e87b2936dc5b1102ffe5376b283af6bab5f/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/protocol/oidc/endpoints/LoginStatusIframeEndpoint.java#L56-L71) that could be re-used to accomplish this for the login status iframe, which need a similar check.

issue_title: Unable to import master realm with --import-realm
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

dist/quarkus

### Describe the bug

Importing the master realm is impossible with --import-realm cli option. The reason of it is that we first falsely initialize master realm and then when trying to import it we skip it because it was already created by the previous initialization.

This situation should be handled by this code: https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/866101e72e8d45a52980cdab9a121c661df039e7/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/services/resources/KeycloakApplication.java#L151

and master realm should not be initialized. However, this does not work for `--import-realm` only for import/export actions.

Here is the relevant log:
```
2024-09-05 12:17:43,611 INFO  [org.keycloak.exportimport.dir.DirImportProvider] (main) Importing from directory /opt/keycloak/bin/../data/import
2024-09-05 12:17:51,461 INFO  [org.keycloak.services] (main) KC-SERVICES0030: Full model import requested. Strategy: IGNORE_EXISTING
2024-09-05 12:18:02,211 INFO  [org.keycloak.exportimport.util.ImportUtils] (main) Realm 'master' already exists. Import skipped
2024-09-05 12:18:47,396 INFO  [org.keycloak.exportimport.util.ImportUtils] (main) Realm 'quarkus' imported
2024-09-05 12:18:48,451 INFO  [org.keycloak.services] (main) KC-SERVICES0032: Import finished successfully
```

### Version

25.0.2

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

master realm is imported

### Actual behavior

master realm is skipped

### How to Reproduce?

1. Put json with master realm to `data/import/`
[master-realm.json](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/16893491/master-realm.json)
2. Run Keycloak with 
```
./kc.sh start-dev --import-realm
```
3. Check that the master realm does not have `sslRequired: none` 


### Anything else?

This was experienced when running Keycloak in Kubernetes with HTTP (for demo purposes). The admin console is not accessible unless sslRequired is set to none.
commit_message:
fix: allows for the detection of a master realm with --import-realms (#32914)

also moving initial bootstrapping after import

closes: #32689

issue_title: CVE-2024-8391 - Allocation of Resources Without Limits or Throttling in io.vertx:vertx-grpc-client
issue_body:
CVE-2024-8391 - Allocation of Resources Without Limits or Throttling in io.vertx:vertx-grpc-client
io.vertx:vertx-grpc-client
Introduced through: org.keycloak:keycloak-quarkus-server-deployment@999.0.0-SNAPSHOT › org.keycloak:keycloak-quarkus-server@999.0.0-SNAPSHOT › io.quarkus:quarkus-opentelemetry@3.13.3 › io.vertx:vertx-grpc-client@4.5.9
## Overview

Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Allocation of Resources Without Limits or Throttling due to the lack of a maximum message size limit. An attacker can cause a denial of service by sending excessively large messages.

**Note** This vulnerability does not affect the Vert.x gRPC server based grpc-java and Netty libraries (Maven GAV: `io.vertx:vertx-grpc`)
## Remediation
Upgrade `io.vertx:vertx-grpc-client` to version 4.5.10 or higher.
## References
- [GitHub Commit](https://github.com/eclipse-vertx/vertx-grpc/commit/a76b14a92410c89fcc590c5852d800b565916ccf)
- [GitHub Issue](https://github.com/eclipse-vertx/vertx-grpc/issues/113)
- [Gitlab Issue](https://gitlab.eclipse.org/security/cve-assignement/-/issues/31)

issue_title: KeyCloak Admin Client uses non-standard `@NoCache` annotation which is an issue for Quarkus
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/client-java

### Describe the bug

As reported by @famod [here](https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/issues/42413), the KeyCloak Admin Client started using the non-standard RESTEasy `@NoCache` application in 25+, which is a problem with Quarkus:

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/commit/3fffc5182ebf93225b3da40fce5d945e04155cdf#diff-cfcc9e857008e4393a63a51cada94d238d4aecd610d4742fde9965fa0f4ef754R27

In Quarkus, we can use the KeyCloak Admin Client with RESTEasy but it can also be used with Quarkus REST (which doesn't support the RESTEasy-specific `@NoCache` annotation).

In the KeyCloak Admin Client, only standard JAX-RS features should be used if we want it to work with Quarkus REST.

I'm not entirely sure how `@NoCache` behavior can be implemented on the client wtih standard JAX-RS features but I would expect it to be unnecessary if the server specifies `@NoCache`?

### Version

25.0.0

### Regression

- [X] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

KeyCloak should only use standard JAX-RS annotations.

### Actual behavior

KeyCloak uses RESTEasy-specific annotations.

### How to Reproduce?

Running any of these Quarkus tests would lead to a warning about the missing annotation:

- io.quarkus.keycloak.admin.client.reactive.KeycloakAdminClientInjectionDevServicesTest
- io.quarkus.it.keycloak.StaticTenantConfigPolicyEnforcerTest
- io.quarkus.it.keycloak.DynamicTenantConfigPolicyEnforcerTest
- and quarkus:build in the same module as the two ITs above: quarkus-integration-test-keycloak-authorization

### Anything else?

https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/issues/42413
commit_message:
Remove @NoCache annotation in admin client interfaces

Closes #32731

Signed-off-by: stianst <stianst@gmail.com>

issue_title: The endpoint /admin/realms/{{realm}}/groups/{{group-id}}/members potentially fetch all user in database
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/api

### Describe the bug

The endpoint /admin/realms/{{realm}}/groups/{{group-id}}/members accept three arguments, first, max and briefRepresentation. 
When using "first" to offset the users retrieved, Keycloak fetch all the "first" number of users instead of retrieving the "max" number of user at the "first" offset.

 Looking at the code the problem seems to be in `org.keycloak.storage.UserStorageManager#getGroupMembersStream(org.keycloak.models.RealmModel, org.keycloak.models.GroupModel, java.lang.Integer, java.lang.Integer) `where the overload `org.keycloak.storage.UserStorageManager#query(org.keycloak.storage.UserStorageManager.PaginatedQuery, org.keycloak.models.RealmModel, java.lang.Integer, java.lang.Integer)` is called instead of this one `org.keycloak.storage.UserStorageManager#query(org.keycloak.storage.UserStorageManager.PaginatedQuery, org.keycloak.storage.UserStorageManager.CountQuery, org.keycloak.models.RealmModel, java.lang.Integer, java.lang.Integer)`

This result in the first argument of the query method (the paginated query used to actually retrive the users) being used to count the number of users the provider can provide.

On a large database and an offset big enough this result in an OOM.

### Version

25.0.5

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Calling /admin/realms/{{realm}}/groups/{{group-id}}/members with the first argument does not fetch "first" + 1 users in database but instead fetch "max" users at "first" offset

### Actual behavior

Calling /admin/realms/{{realm}}/groups/{{group-id}}/members does return the excepted result but internally fetch the all the "first" users, resulting in OOM with a large "first" offset 

### How to Reproduce?

Start keycloak with a limited amount of memory and a realm with a large number of users. Make a call to /admin/realms/{{realm}}/groups/{{group-id}}/members with the first parameter being set to the number of users in database.
If the number of user is large enough keycloak will OOM, take a very long time.
Activating hibernate SQL logging will show that keycloak retrieve  all users in database

### Anything else?

org.keycloak.storage.UserStorageManager#searchForUserStream calls the correct UserStorageManager#query, maybe getGroupMembersStream should do the same ?
commit_message:
Fix UserStorageManager.getGroupMembersStream potentially fetching all user (#33145)

Closes #32761
Signed-off-by: Benoit Messager <benoit.messager@liksi.fr>
Co-authored-by: Benoit Messager <benoit.messager@liksi.fr>

issue_title: When forcing re-authentication by passing maxAge value as 0 does not work
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

oidc

### Describe the bug

Standard behavior to force user re-authentication when the user is trying to access some sensetive part of the the application is to re-authenticate the user by passing max_age=0 in the authentication url for keycloak or any other open-id provider.

However due to the below code in keycloak.js the value 0 gets treated as false hence the url parameter is not sent out.
`        if (options && options.maxAge) {
            url += '&max_age=' + encodeURIComponent(options.maxAge);
}`

Currently I'm working this around by passing maxAge:1 or maxAge:-1 and it works, but using 1 second might still leave some vulnerabilities and -1 may not be supported by all oidc providers.

Note that I have tried using maxAge:0 with oidc-client library and it works there.

### Version

25.0.5

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

when maxAge:0 is passed keycloak.js should pass it ahead in the auth url as max_age=0

### Actual behavior

currently maxAge 0 gets treated as false and max_age parameter is not sent.

### How to Reproduce?

Try calling
keycloak.login({
          scope: `openid`,
          redirectUri: `${loginUrl}`,
          maxAge: 0,
        });

### Anything else?
Work around:
keycloak.login({
          scope: `openid`,
          redirectUri: `${loginUrl}`,
          maxAge: 1,
        });
OR
keycloak.login({
          scope: `openid`,
          redirectUri: `${loginUrl}`,
          maxAge: -1,
        });
currently does work, but may have other problems.

_No response_
commit_message:
Allow a `maxAge` of  `0` when building a login URL (#32855)

Closes #32764

Signed-off-by: Karan Shukla <karan.shukla@sarvaha.com>

issue_title: Realm import fails when client configures default_acr values
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

import-export

### Describe the bug

Importing an exported json realm configuration fails if it contains a custom acr_to_loa mapping and a client that references the acr value via default_acr_values.

### Version

Keycloak 25.0.5

### Regression

- [X] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Import should work fine.

This issue is present in KC25 and KC24

### Actual behavior

Import fails with an exception:


```
2024-09-11 12:33:48,828 ERROR [org.keycloak.services.error.KeycloakErrorHandler] (executor-thread-19) Uncaught server error: java.lang.RuntimeException: Invalid client client-silver: Default ACR values need to contain values specified in the ACR-To-Loa mapping or number levels from set realm browser flow
	at org.keycloak.storage.datastore.DefaultExportImportManager.lambda$createClients$7(DefaultExportImportManager.java:541)
	at org.keycloak.validation.ValidationUtil.validateClient(ValidationUtil.java:45)
	at org.keycloak.validation.ValidationUtil.validateClient(ValidationUtil.java:28)
	at org.keycloak.storage.datastore.DefaultExportImportManager.createClients(DefaultExportImportManager.java:540)
	at org.keycloak.storage.datastore.DefaultExportImportManager.importRealm(DefaultExportImportManager.java:369)
	at org.keycloak.models.utils.RepresentationToModel.importRealm(RepresentationToModel.java:139)
	at org.keycloak.services.managers.RealmManager.importRealm(RealmManager.java:584)
	at org.keycloak.services.managers.RealmManager.importRealm(RealmManager.java:513)
	at org.keycloak.services.managers.RealmManagerProviderFactory.lambda$postInit$0(RealmManagerProviderFactory.java:54)
	at org.keycloak.services.DefaultKeycloakSessionFactory.publish(DefaultKeycloakSessionFactory.java:91)
	at org.keycloak.storage.ImportRealmFromRepresentationEvent.fire(ImportRealmFromRepresentationEvent.java:50)
	at org.keycloak.storage.datastore.DefaultExportImportManager.importRealm(DefaultExportImportManager.java:172)
	at org.keycloak.services.resources.admin.RealmsAdminResource.importRealm(RealmsAdminResource.java:147)
	at org.keycloak.services.resources.admin.RealmsAdminResource$quarkusrestinvoker$importRealm_aa9defcbd5e1fb410aecd3ab57b280946687671e.invoke(Unknown Source)
	at org.jboss.resteasy.reactive.server.handlers.InvocationHandler.handle(InvocationHandler.java:29)
	at io.quarkus.resteasy.reactive.server.runtime.QuarkusResteasyReactiveRequestContext.invokeHandler(QuarkusResteasyReactiveRequestContext.java:141)
	at org.jboss.resteasy.reactive.common.core.AbstractResteasyReactiveContext.run(AbstractResteasyReactiveContext.java:147)
	at io.quarkus.vertx.core.runtime.VertxCoreRecorder$14.runWith(VertxCoreRecorder.java:635)
	at org.jboss.threads.EnhancedQueueExecutor$Task.doRunWith(EnhancedQueueExecutor.java:2516)
	at org.jboss.threads.EnhancedQueueExecutor$Task.run(EnhancedQueueExecutor.java:2495)
	at org.jboss.threads.EnhancedQueueExecutor$ThreadBody.run(EnhancedQueueExecutor.java:1521)
	at org.jboss.threads.DelegatingRunnable.run(DelegatingRunnable.java:11)
	at org.jboss.threads.ThreadLocalResettingRunnable.run(ThreadLocalResettingRunnable.java:11)
	at io.netty.util.concurrent.FastThreadLocalRunnable.run(FastThreadLocalRunnable.java:30)
	at java.base/java.lang.Thread.run(Thread.java:840)

```

### How to Reproduce?

1) Create a realm
2) Create a acr-to-loa mapping in the realm, e.g. silver: 1 and gold: 2
3) Create a client
4) In advanced client settings configure "Default ACR Values to silver"
5) Export the realm
6) Delete the realm
7) Try to reimport the realm json

### Anything else?

The problem seems to be that the realm attributes are not yet set in org.keycloak.storage.datastore.DefaultExportImportManager#importRealm(org.keycloak.representations.idm.RealmRepresentation, org.keycloak.models.RealmModel, boolean)
when the client configuration is imported. However the `acr.loa.map` realm attribute is needed to validate the client configuration.

I propose to move the realm attribute import above the client import.
This solves the issue.

An example file that reproduces the error can be found here: https://gist.github.com/thomasdarimont/b0928cf6d6df07dc76d52413794a1414
commit_message:
Ensure realm attributes import happens before client import

Fixes #32799

Signed-off-by: Thomas Darimont <thomas.darimont@googlemail.com>

issue_title: Requested `grant_types` inconsistent with created `grant_types` for OpenID Connect Dynamic Client Registration
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

oidc

### Describe the bug

When specifying `grant_types` in a OpenID Connect Dynamic Client Registration the requested and returned `grant_types` do not match. 

### Version

nightly

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Requested and returned `grant_types` are the same

### Actual behavior

Requested `grant_types` results in different created `grant-types`:

* `authorization_code` --> `authorization_code` [OK]
* `client_credentials` --> `[authorization_code, client_credentials]` [NOT OK]
* `password` --> `authorization_code` [NOT OK]
* `urn:ietf:params:oauth:grant-type:device_code` --> `authorization_code` [NOT OK]

### How to Reproduce?

Use `kct` to dynamically create clients for different flows, using the following command replacing flows with browser, client, password, and device, to try the different grant types:

```
kct config context create --iss myissuer -c mycontext --create-client --flow <FLOW> -X
```

Before running the above you need to create an issuer in `kct`, and context with a flow that uses boostrap admin user or client, and register that context with the issuer as the `client-registration-context`.

Using `-X` option you will see the request sent to Keycloak, and the responses.

### Anything else?

_No response_

Requested grant_types inconsistent with created grant_types for OpenID Connect Dynamic Client Registration
closes #32801

issue_title: Lightweight access token is not working for bootstrap admin client
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

oidc

### Describe the bug

I tested out https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/32347 with the bootstrap admin client, and ran into some issues: 

* Bootstrap admin client doesn't use lightweight tokens, which it should by default
* When enabling `Always use lightweight access token` for the client I get a 403

### Version

nightly

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Bootstrap admin client uses lightweight access token by default, and works out of the box

### Actual behavior

Uses regular tokens by default, and results in a 403 if `Always use lightweight access token` is enabled for the client. I suspect this is down to the fact that in `https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/32347` it states it uses the presence of the `sub` claim to determine if it's a lightweight access token or not, but the token I get with `Always use lightweight access token` includes the sub claim:

```
$ kct token -c admin -d
{
  "exp" : 1726053554,
  "iat" : 1726053494,
  "jti" : "286867a7-e822-4a2c-b3e8-4355d917117a",
  "iss" : "http://localhost:8080/realms/master",
  "sub" : "eb7bfd3a-bb09-477f-94d9-e0fa2ff0ee05",
  "typ" : "Bearer",
  "azp" : "temp-admin",
  "scope" : "profile email"
}
```

### How to Reproduce?

Start Keycloak with `--bootstrap-admin-client-secret=mysecret` and grab a token for that client, decode it and see that it contains all claims. Enable `Always use lightweight access token` for the client, and try to invoke the Admin REST APIs with it, and you'll get a 403.

Another thing is that it seems the `sub` claim should not be present in a lightweight access token by default, but it still is:
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/df7be101-0d21-4563-b209-fe0b6ab6b444)


### Anything else?

_No response_
